raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, while on vacation in XXXX, XXXX, I went to fill up at a gas station, where I handed my credit card ending in XXXX to the gas attendant who had a HANDHELD CREDIT CARD MACHINE, I assumed that the HANDHELD CREDIT CARD MACHINE was for the GAS STATION and no other business. He then proceeded to run my card on the handheld credit card machine that I thought was for the gas station and then returned it back to me stating my card was declined. I was not given proof of said declined charge. As a result, I proceeded to pay with cash where I was not provided with a receipt for cash payment. I was not aware this handheld credit card machine was for a business named XXXX XXXX XXXX  from XXXX XXXX . I DID NOT authorize payment of {$590.00} to this said merchant, only to the gas station XXXX. 
Again, although I DID hand my credit card for in person face to face charge, I was made to believe that the handheld credit card machine was for the gas station XXXX. I was not informed that handheld credit card machine was for another business. 

It is impossible to believe that I may be the first person that his happens to where people hand over their credit cards to be charged on a handheld machine, be told it was declined and later find out card was charged for a transaction that was not authorized. 

I am very displeased with Barclays asking for proof of payment with other means, as I stated before, when I was informed my card was declined ( with no proof provided ), I paid cash and was not provided a receipt for cash payment. I re-state, I can not be the first one that isnt provided a receipt for cash payment. 

Your letter dated XX/XX/year> mentions that Merchant has not violated the terms and conditions of the contract. I have called Barclays asking for the contract and the contact information of the merchant so I can then dispute the charge with the merchant directly. Barclays fails to provide this information. This is also unacceptable.
